what_it_does: Stylesheet for the right-hand-man (RHM) overlay UI: a persistent draggable 'V' handle, an open-fan scrim and verb menu, a first-contact greeting card, a brain/chat panel with starter chips and a guided-walk stepper, a note composer, and an open V-post composer. It defines layout, tokens-driven color, typography, shadows, focus rings, animations, and z-index layering.
observations: Uses only CSS custom properties (tokens) for color, spacing, radii, shadows, and typography; no hardcoded colors.
Defines explicit z-index layering: scrim 44, handle 45, notices/gallery above, greeting/brain/note panels at 46.
Handle is fixed bottom-right, 56px square, pill-shaped, with grab/grabbing cursor and touch-action: none.
Fan verbs are absolutely positioned from the icon center and use inline transforms for the radial arc.
Live verbs are two-line cards with a label and description; 'soon' verbs are compact dashed-row pills with a status chip.
First-contact greeting card has a CSS tail pointing down at the V handle.
Brain panel includes starter question chips and a guided-walk stepper UI.
Note composer has an explicit 'honest-unavailable' state for aims that cannot accept a note.
Open V-post composer is a collapsed toggle with channel selector, message textarea, and send button.
Includes prefers-reduced-motion media query that disables animations.
standouts: Token-only coloring with a stated 'zero hardcoded colour' contract.
Detailed z-index rationale in comments tying the RHM to scrubber, bottom sheet, notices, and gallery modal.
First-contact greeting tail visually anchors the card to the V handle.
'Soon' verbs use dashed borders and a status chip instead of disabled opacity or misleading active styling.
Brain panel combines starter chips, a guided tour stepper, and a slot for forked brain UI.
conventions: CSS custom properties for all visual values; px used only for geometric sizes like the 56px handle and radial arc.
Fixed positioning with safe-area inset var(--safe-b) and repeated bottom offset pattern.
Focus-visible outlines with token color and 2px/3px offsets.
Comments double as design rationale and composition contracts.
concerns: Several magic numbers are repeated (56px handle size, 22px tail offset, 208/300/320px widths) rather than tokenized.
Bottom offset calc is duplicated across .v-greet, .v-brain-panel, and .v-note; a shared token would reduce drift.
.v-brain-panel toggles visibility only via display:none/flex with no aria-hidden semantics visible in CSS.
.v-post-send:disabled uses a hardcoded opacity 0.5 instead of a token.
Long explanatory comments increase file size and may drift from code if not maintained.
notes: This file is the complete visual layer for the RHM assistant overlay—handle, fan, greeting, brain, note, and post surfaces—built entirely from design tokens with explicit accessibility and layering rules.
questions: Which JS component toggles data-open and data-greet and computes the fan verb transforms?
What tokens are defined in tokens/paper.css and are all referenced variables guaranteed to exist?
Is the 56px handle size defined as a token elsewhere, or is it intentionally a geometric constant?
How is the list of 'soon' verbs maintained and surfaced to users?
What drives the guided-walk stepper state machine and the starter question chips?
What is the relationship between .v-brain-panel and the forked .v-brain classes it is meant to re-skin?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it styles the RHM handle and its related overlay surfaces as a persistent, token-driven, layered assistant UI.
apparent_themes: Persistent assistant organism anchored to the viewport corner
intention_signals: The file is trying to build a cohesive, paper-ethos right-hand-man assistant that is always available, visually anchored, honest about what is live vs. coming soon, accessible, and layered cleanly above the rest of the app without taking over.
novelty: A persistent 'V' handle with a radial fan of verbs, an aim caption, a greeting card with a CSS tail pointing at the handle, provisional 'soon' verb pills, and a brain panel that mixes starter chips, a guided tour stepper, and forked UI.
contribution: A complete, token-based CSS surface for a persistent assistant overlay, including handle, fan, scrim, greeting, brain panel, note composer, and post composer, with explicit z-index layering, focus-visible accessibility, and reduced-motion support.
summary_for_embedding: CSS stylesheet defining the right-hand-man overlay UI (draggable V handle, fan menu, scrim, greeting, brain panel, note composer, and post composer) using only design tokens, with explicit z-index layering, focus-visible accessibility, and reduced-motion support.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a cohesive, token-driven visual and interaction layer for a persistent right-hand-man assistant that stays legible, accessible, and honest about provisional features.
